ocr.text: Type  Number  Placement Criteria  Total Consumption (mA)  Comments
Opening sensor  8  1/window 1/airflow door  -  It requires KNX interface
Presence detector  7  1/room  56  Installation height = 2.20 m
Motion detector  4  1/passing area  16  It includes bus coupler and connection terminal
Temperature sensor  6  1/room  -
Luminaires actuator  12  It depends on the luminaires number  75  Binary input to dry contacts connection or conventional push button
Heating electrovalve  6  1/room  5.2
Split control interface  4  1/split  -  Bidirectional KNX gateway—Air conditioners
BP index  Time period for blood pressure assessment  RR (95% CI)  Pvalue
Diastolic BP (per 10 mmHg)  Baseline  1.07 (0.94–1.21)  0.30
Systolic BP (per 10 mmHg)  Baseline  1.09 (1.01–1.17)  0.02
Pulse pressure(per 10 mmHg)  Baseline  1.12 (1.02–1.23)  0.02
The first table outlines various types of sensors and devices with their respective number, placement criteria, total consumption
(mA), and comments: the opening sensor has 8 units, with a placement of 1/window 1/airflow door, requiring a KNX interface;
the presence detector consists of 7 units, placed 1/room, with a total consumption of 56 mA and an installation height of 2.20 m;
the motion detector includes 4 units, for 1/passing area, consuming 16 mA and featuring a bus coupler and connection terminal;
the temperature sensor has 6 units, placed 1/room, with no specified consumption; the luminaires actuator comprises 12 units,
depending on the luminaires number, with a total consumption of 75 mA and a binary input for dry contacts connection or
conventional push button; the heating electrovalve has 6 units, placed 1/room, consuming 5.2 mA; and the split control interface
features 4 units, placed 1/split, with no specified consumption and serving as a bidirectional KNX gateway for air conditioners.
The second table presents blood pressure assessment data, indicating the diastolic BP (per 10 mmHg) has a baseline RR of 1.07
(0.94–1.21) and a Pvalue of 0.30, with a mean follow-up RR of 1.20 (0.97–1.49) and a Pvalue of 0.09. The systolic BP (per 10
mmHg) shows a baseline RR of 1.09 (1.01–1.17) with a Pvalue of 0.02 and a mean follow-up RR of 1.30 (1.15–1.48) with a
Pvalue of <0.01. Finally, the pulse pressure (per 10 mmHg) has a baseline RR of 1.12 (1.02–1.23) and a Pvalue of 0.02, with a
mean follow-up RR of 1.28 (1.11–1.47) and a Pvalue of <0.01.
